items.summary: Perform a deep audit and full rewrite of the AdminGuard component and the backend claimAdmin / checkAdminStatus flow. The fix must address: (1) After a successful Internet Identity login, if no admin principal has been registered in the backend yet, the authenticated user must see a clearly visible 'Claim Admin Access' button — not an 'Access Denied' message. (2) Clicking that button must call the backend `claimAdmin` function, correctly handle all return variants (success, alreadyClaimed, notAuthenticated, and any __kind__-tagged variants), and on success immediately re-fetch admin status and redirect to /admin dashboard. (3) If an admin is already registered and the current user is NOT that admin, show 'Access Denied' with no claim button. (4) Ensure the component never incorrectly treats a freshly authenticated identity as unauthenticated due to actor caching, stale identity, or race conditions between auth state and actor instantiation. Invalidate and refetch admin-status queries immediately after login completes and after a successful claimAdmin call. (5) Verify the backend `claimAdmin` stores the caller's principal when adminPrincipal is null/None, and that `checkAdminStatus` returns true for that principal on all subsequent calls. (6) The fix must work correctly in both Safari and Chrome browsers.
